label: neither
rationale: Endorsement of Matiangi as a political remedy for corruption and gang violence; no attack on any group - 'dawa ya goons kenya ni matiangi' targets a problem, not a community.

label: hate
rationale: 'Mjaluo has a sore throat shouting on an empty stomach' and 'mkaale hajawai pata barabara' collectively stereotype both Luo and Kalenjin communities as politically exploited fools who gain nothing - contemptuous collective characterisation of two ethnic groups.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Maumbwa nyinyi' directed at police/goons as individuals or an institutional group, not at an ethnic community.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Chief thief' is a personal insult aimed at a named individual, Kasongo, with no ethnic targeting.

label: neither
rationale: Factual political observation about a neutralisation strategy targeting Wamunyoro as a political actor, not collective contempt for the community.

label: neither
rationale: Factual historical claim about land in Ol Kalou with reference to Samburu name origins; critical but directed at specific 'guys' rather than collectively attributing a trait to the Samburu community.

label: neither
rationale: French-language speech by a military official criticising Kinshasa residents for littering and threatening civic re-education; targets a civic behaviour, not a Kenyan ethnic community, and falls outside the NCIC framing of this task.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Very stupid people' is personal abuse of Kasongo and associates as individuals, with no ethnic dimension.

label: neither
rationale: Political criticism of unnamed Kenyan actors who supported the JKIA-Adani deal; no attack on an ethnic or national-origin group as a group.

label: hate
rationale: 'Deny these mafia a Kikuyu controlled government' frames the entire Kikuyu community as a mafia power bloc seeking illegitimate control - collective attribution of criminal political intent to the ethnic group.

label: hate
rationale: 'Muslim will anti islam' and 'Luo will spark anti nilotic in 60% almost bantu country' frames a coming ethnic/religious backlash as natural and inevitable, stirring inter-community contempt with the Bantu/Nilotic divide.

label: neither
rationale: Celebratory account of voters in Ol Kalou defying intimidation; 'siasa ya ukora imeanguka' criticises corrupt politics, not any ethnic community.

label: hate
rationale: 'Wewe kwa wale watu wote wa Mount Kenya ur the worst judas iscariot' transfers a betrayal label onto the entire Mount Kenya community through the addressee, holding the group collectively responsible.

label: neither
rationale: 'Shetani, umaskini na kasongo' - three enemies listed; Kasongo is named as an individual political adversary, not an ethnic group.

label: hate
rationale: 'Entitled kikuyus... stole 2007 presidential election' and 'Kalenjins do not need Entitled kikuyus and their deception' collectively attributes electoral theft and deception to Kikuyus as a group - NCIC ethnic contempt.

label: hate
rationale: '2027 is payback time on kikuyus... kikuyus who resist will see Fire' is an explicit ethnic violence threat targeting Kikuyus as a group in retribution framing.

label: neither
rationale: 'Itabidi mrudi mstari wa mbele kwa maandamano' warns Luo Nyanza supporters they will bear political costs if Ruto is re-elected; political consequence framing directed at supporters of a politician, not collective contempt for the Luo community.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Stupid tribalist' is personal abuse directed at a named individual (Wamunyoro walker), not transferred onto his community.

label: neither
rationale: Ethnic-bloc electoral arithmetic listing Maasai, Luhya, Kamba, and Kisii voting patterns with no contempt or attack.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Wahuni wakubwa nyie', 'wachochezi' - generic insults directed at protest organisers as individuals, no ethnic dimension.

label: hate
rationale: 'You were taught kikuyus are the reason you don't have a tarmac road' describes and subtly reinforces the anti-Kikuyu collective blame narrative directed at the addressee, reproducing ethnic contempt even while framing it critically.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kununua wakikuyu' (buying Kikuyus) collectively attributes venality and electoral betrayal to Kikuyus as a group - 'unawapea 1k every day, wanachukua alafu wanasema watavote for your competitor'.

label: hate
rationale: 'Why are most Luos suddenly so clever and philosophical?' uses mocking irony to ridicule Luo intellectual capacity as a collective trait - NCIC-standard ethnic ridicule.

label: neither
rationale: Historical reference to Mau Mau targeting collaborators, framed as a critique of a named politician (Ichungwa), not an attack on any ethnic community.

label: hate
rationale: 'We know how controlling women from that region are' attributes a negative collective character trait to women of an implied regional/ethnic community - NCIC ethnic contempt without a violence call.
